name: Piper J Gallery
self_description: Piper J Gallery is a welcoming art venue located in Truckee, CA showcasing the work by a wide variety of art and artists. Since it's establishment in 2016, Piper J Gallery has been a place where collectors can discover contemporary art for modern mountain living.

name: Riverside Studios
self_description: Riverside Studios is a unique art gallery and gift shop in downtown Truckee. It is owned and operated by four woman artists: Mary Guerra, Alanna Hughes, Kahlil Johnson and Sondrea Larsen.

name: Steve Randall Community Recreation Center
self_description: The Steve Randall Community Recreation Center is a multi-use facility complete with a gymnasium, fitness center, climbing wall, dance room, meeting rooms, kitchen, and more.
notes: found on home page; descriptive but not first-person narrative

name: Truckee Dance Factory
self_description: Truckee Dance Factory (TDF) is the premier dance studio serving the North Lake Tahoe area. Offering a variety of programs for students ages 10 months and up, TDF inspires a love of dance in a supportive space that fosters creativity, self-confidence, and self-discipline.

name: Truckee Railroad Museum
self_description: The Truckee Railroad Museum is located in a former Southern Pacific Railroad bay-window caboose next to the historic railroad depot in downtown Truckee.
notes: descriptive but not first-person; venue does not provide mission or 'we are' statement

name: Truckee Tahoe Airport
notes: page content is about Art at the Airport program, not airport self-description

name: Bespoke & Atelier
notes: page shows store info and yarn selection but no clear self-description; mentions 'we are open everyday' but lacks substantive identity statement

name: Bluestone Jewelry & Wine
self_description: Opened in 2007 in Tahoe city Linda's favorite jewelry store was closing. As many people, they came originally for skiing Marc and Linda decided to take a chance and purchased the storefront.

name: Buoy & Trap Seafood Market
self_description: Owned and operated by a native Rhode Island seafood connoisseur with years of experience in the food and beverage industry, we are bringing a unique and bespoke product to the Truckee Tahoe region.

name: Cabona's
self_description: Cabona's has been part of Truckee's story since 1906, when Dave Cabona opened a Dry Goods Emporium stocked with everything mountain life required. As the town evolved, so did we, trading in rubber and reels for flannel and fashion, bringing a touch of style to Sierra living.

name: California 89
self_description: California 89 was born from a love of adventure and a deep connection to the Sierra. Rooted in the Wild. Built by Family.

name: Cultural Threads
self_description: Connecting you with artisans at 14,000 ft. in the Peruvian Andes. Cultural Threads empowers indigenous women to preserve natural alpaca fibers and handspun traditions by creating sustainable knits that are good for humanity and the environment.

name: Echo Blue
self_description: Locally owned and operated in Truckee, CA, Echo Blue introduces mountain chic to up-cycled women's fashion.

name: Hello Clogs
self_description: Hello Clogs is a small-batch line of handmade wooden clogs crafted in the foothills of Nevada City, California by designer and maker Carabeth Rowley. Hello Clogs celebrates traditional techniques, modern design, and everyday wearability.
